meta.title: GitHub Automation Subsystem — Technical Process Design (SSOT)
meta.purpose: Single source of truth for the design and implementation of the GitHub automation subsystem. All workflows, artifacts, gates, and process steps are derived from this document. No implementation work begins until open questions are resolved and status advances to APPROVED.

prior_analysis_sources: GIT_OPS/Second Architecture Delta for GitHub Hardening.md

governing_laws.name: APP_FIRST_AUTHENTICATION
governing_laws.statement: All automation that reads or writes GitHub Projects must authenticate via a GitHub App installation token. GITHUB_TOKEN cannot access Projects. PATs are permitted only as an explicitly declared exception with a named owner and expiry date.
governing_laws.rationale: GITHUB_TOKEN is repository-scoped and cannot access Projects v2. PATs tied to individuals create bus-factor and rotation risk. GitHub App tokens are ephemeral (1-hour max), scope-limited, and do not depend on any individual account.
governing_laws.enforcement: GATE_GH_APP_AUTH_AVAILABLE must pass before any workflow that touches Projects fields runs.

governing_laws.name: NO_INLINE_FIELD_IDS
governing_laws.statement: No workflow, script, or ProgramSpec may hardcode a GitHub Project node ID, field ID, or option/iteration ID. All IDs must be resolved at runtime from config/github_field_registry.json.
governing_laws.rationale: Field IDs are stable until a field is deleted and recreated or the project is recreated. Hardcoded IDs create silent failures after project migration or recreation. Query-then-extract at runtime makes the system survivable.
governing_laws.enforcement: GATE_GH_FIELD_REGISTRY_VALID must pass. Any workflow that reads a field ID must source it from github_field_registry.json. Linting rule in bootstrap-github-fields.yml checks for hardcoded PVTF_/PVTSSF_/PVTIF_ strings in workflow files.

governing_laws.name: PHASE_ADVANCEMENT_IS_COMPOSITE
governing_laws.statement: Phase transitions on the GitHub Project Phase field may only be made by the Phase Advancement Evaluator, backed by a stored phase_transition_decision.json artifact. No workflow may update the Phase field based on a single event.
governing_laws.rationale: Phase advancement requires composite evaluation of gate passage, issue closure, and evidence presence. Single-event triggers (PR merged → advance phase) are edge-triggered and create permanent divergence when events are missed.
governing_laws.enforcement: GATE_GH_PHASE_ADVANCEMENT_DECIDED is required before Phase field mutation. Phase field is listed under automation_only_fields in the sync governance policy.

governing_laws.name: PLATFORM_SPLIT_REQUIRED
governing_laws.statement: Every ProgramSpec and workflow must declare its required runner class. GitHub-hosted ubuntu-latest is the default for all GraphQL/REST operations, gate evaluation, and plan projection. Self-hosted Windows runners are used only for Windows-specific toolchain operations. Any workflow targeting a self-hosted runner must declare a fallback strategy.
governing_laws.rationale: The codebase has mixed Windows and Linux assumptions. Uncontrolled platform assignment creates environmental drift and untestable workflows.
governing_laws.enforcement: policies/runner_policy.yaml defines category-to-runner mapping. Missing runner_class in a ProgramSpec is a schema validation error.

governing_laws.name: PROJECTION_IS_UNIDIRECTIONAL
governing_laws.statement: The plan SSOT (YAML/JSON files in the repo) is the sole authority for decomposition, step contracts, phase structure, gate requirements, and evidence requirements. GitHub is the execution surface. No automation may write plan-owned fields back from GitHub to the plan files.
governing_laws.rationale: Bidirectional sync between SSOT and execution surfaces reliably produces data loss and unresolvable conflicts. Documented failure mode across Linear, custom integrations, and the cross-system analysis.

governing_laws.name: NO_ISSUE_WITHOUT_STEP_ID
governing_laws.statement: Every GitHub Issue that represents execution work must carry a StepID project field value matching an entry in config/step_issue_linkage.json. Issues without a StepID are classified as Orphan drift by the reconciliation loop.
governing_laws.rationale: Free-floating issues bypass the plan, break traceability, and make phase advancement evaluation unreliable.
governing_laws.enforcement: ssot-drift-reconciliation.yml classifies StepID-absent issues as Orphan drift and opens a flag issue referencing the orphan.

governing_laws.name: NO_PR_WITHOUT_ISSUE
governing_laws.statement: Every pull request must reference a GitHub Issue via 'Closes #N' or 'Fixes #N' in the PR body. The referenced issue must exist in step_issue_linkage.json.
governing_laws.rationale: PRs that do not map to step contracts bypass governance and make gate enforcement meaningless.
governing_laws.enforcement: TEMP_branch-handler.yml (renamed to branch-handler.yml) validates PR body on opened/edited events and blocks merge if linkage is absent.

governing_laws.name: NO_MERGE_WITHOUT_GATE
governing_laws.statement: All gate workflows listed in the plan's gate_requirements for the active phase must produce passing required status checks before a PR can be merged.
governing_laws.rationale: Gates are the enforcement mechanism for the phase contract. Optional gates defeat the governance model.
governing_laws.enforcement: Branch protection rules on the default branch require all gate status checks. Gate list is derived from the active phase's gate_requirements at projection time.

architecture.pattern: observe-diff-act-report reconciliation loop (Kubernetes controller pattern applied to project management)
architecture.trigger_model: level-triggered — reconciliation runs on schedule regardless of events; missed webhooks do not create permanent divergence

principles.name: level_triggered_not_edge_triggered
principles.statement: The reconciliation loop cares about current state, not what events occurred. Every critical workflow also runs on a schedule so missed webhooks self-heal on the next cycle.

principles.name: idempotent_mutations
principles.statement: addProjectV2ItemById returns the existing item ID if the item already exists. updateProjectV2ItemFieldValue is safe to re-run. All projection operations converge to the same result regardless of how many times they execute.

principles.name: checksum_based_change_detection
principles.statement: PlanHash (SHA256 of canonical plan package) and ProjectionVersion (monotonic integer) are stored as Project fields. Reconciliation diffs desired vs observed values and skips mutation when they match. Reduces API consumption and eliminates reconciliation storms.

principles.name: two_step_mutation
principles.statement: GitHub Projects v2 requires separate GraphQL calls to add an item (addProjectV2ItemById) and update its fields (updateProjectV2ItemFieldValue). These must never be combined into one call.

principles.name: escape_hatches_required
principles.statement: The Override drift class exists for intentional, approved divergence. A system with no escape hatch forces operators to abandon governance entirely during incidents. Override entries in github_override_registry.json suppress reconciliation correction for named items.

principles.name: fail_closed_on_missing_prerequisites
principles.statement: If github_field_registry.json is absent or fails validation, all downstream workflows that touch Project fields must exit with a non-zero status. Bootstrap must run and pass before projection or reconciliation can proceed.

principles.name: single_writer_authority
principles.statement: Automation-only fields have exactly one writer: the named ProgramSpec for that field. No other workflow, script, or human may write to Phase, PlanHash, ProjectionVersion, EvidenceState, or DeterminismState.

template_to_github_mapping.cardinality: one plan phase = one option in the Phase field
template_to_github_mapping.notes: Phases are NOT milestones. Milestones are delivery checkpoints within a phase.

template_to_github_mapping.cardinality: one step = one issue; sub-steps may become sub-issues up to 2 levels
template_to_github_mapping.notes: StepID project field carries the step_id value. Issue title format: '[STEP-NNN] <contract_summary>'.

template_to_github_mapping.cardinality: one gate = one required status check job
template_to_github_mapping.notes: Gate list per phase is read from the plan package at projection time and written to branch protection rules via GH_PROJECTION_APPLY.

template_to_github_mapping.github_surface: Pull Request linked to an Issue
template_to_github_mapping.cardinality: one PR implements one or more step contracts; PR body must contain 'Closes #N'
template_to_github_mapping.notes: PR is the execution attempt. Merge = contract accepted. branch-handler.yml enforces linkage.

template_to_github_mapping.cardinality: issue closed = step complete; all issues in milestone closed = milestone complete
template_to_github_mapping.writer: GitHub built-in automation on PR merge
template_to_github_mapping.notes: Milestone groups steps that deliver a shippable output. A phase may span multiple milestones. A milestone may not span multiple phases.

template_to_github_mapping.cardinality: one evidence seal = one comment on the linked issue
template_to_github_mapping.writer: post-gate step in CI pipeline; EvidenceState updated by GH_EVIDENCE_SEAL program
template_to_github_mapping.notes: Comment format: 'Evidence sealed: [run_id] — [bundle_path] SHA256: [hash]'. EvidenceState is automation-only.

template_to_github_mapping.cardinality: one milestone_group in plan = one GitHub Milestone
template_to_github_mapping.notes: Milestones are delivery checkpoints only. They track shippable outputs, not lifecycle stages.

process_phases.name: Bootstrap
process_phases.description: One-time setup per project. Discovers and stores all GitHub Project field IDs and option IDs. Must complete successfully before any other process phase can run. Re-runs on demand when the project is recreated or fields are modified.

automatic: On detection of missing or stale github_field_registry.json by any downstream workflow
Scheduled: daily at 00:00 UTC to detect field drift

preconditions: GitHub App installed on the organization/user with read/write Projects permission
App credentials stored as repository secrets: GH_APP_ID, GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY
Project exists and is accessible to the App installation

steps.action: Issue GitHub App installation token
steps.failure_mode: exit non-zero; GATE_GH_APP_AUTH_AVAILABLE fails; no downstream step runs

steps.action: Discover all project fields and options via GraphQL
steps.graphql_query: query($org: String!, $number: Int!) { organization(login: $org) { projectV2(number: $number) { id fields(first: 50) { nodes { ... on ProjectV2Field { id name } ... on ProjectV2SingleSelectField { id name options { id name } } ... on ProjectV2IterationField { id name configuration { iterations { id title startDate duration } } } } } } } }
steps.failure_mode: exit non-zero if required fields are absent from response

steps.action: Validate and write field registry
steps.failure_mode: exit non-zero if any required field name is missing; existing registry is NOT overwritten on failure

steps.action: Commit registry file if changed
steps.notes: Uses a bot commit identity. Commit is skipped if file content is identical to HEAD.

process_phases.name: Plan Projection
process_phases.description: Projects the plan SSOT (YAML/JSON phase plan files) onto GitHub. Creates Issues for all new step contracts, adds them to the Project, sets all automation-only fields, and records stable step↔issue linkage. Runs on every commit that modifies plan files.

automatic: push to default branch where paths match the plan_files_glob pattern (defined in OPEN_QUESTIONS OQ-001)

preconditions: Plan file at canonical path is schema-valid

steps.action: Issue GitHub App installation token
Load field registry and validate
steps.failure_mode: exit non-zero and trigger PROC-0 automatically if registry is absent or stale

steps.action: Generate projection manifest from plan package
inputs: plan package at canonical path (OPEN_QUESTIONS OQ-001)

projection_manifest_fields_per_step.title: string: '[STEP-NNN] <contract_summary>'
projection_manifest_fields_per_step.body: string: full step contract rendered as markdown (inputs, outputs, invariants, gate_requirements)
projection_manifest_fields_per_step.phase: string: phase_id this step belongs to
projection_manifest_fields_per_step.gate_requirements: array of gate IDs required for this step
projection_manifest_fields_per_step.plan_hash: SHA256 of the canonical plan package JSON
projection_manifest_fields_per_step.projection_version: monotonic integer, incremented on each re-projection

steps.action: Apply projection: create/update issues and project items
for_each_step_in_manifest: if step_id NOT in step_issue_linkage.json: create GitHub Issue via REST POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues
addProjectV2ItemById to add issue to project (idempotent: returns existing item_id if already present)
updateProjectV2ItemFieldValue: set Phase = phase option ID (from field registry)
if milestone_group is set: assign issue to milestone (create milestone if absent)
write {step_id: issue_number, item_id, projected_at} to step_issue_linkage.json
for_each_step_already_in_linkage: compare desired PlanHash vs observed PlanHash on project item
if mismatch: update issue body + PlanHash + ProjectionVersion fields
steps.rate_limit_behavior: read rate limit headers after each mutation; sleep if X-RateLimit-Remaining < 10; use exponential backoff with jitter on 429 responses

steps.action: Commit linkage file if changed
steps.commit_message_format: chore(github-automation): update step-issue linkage after projection [PROJ-{run_id}]

outputs: GitHub Issues created for all new step contracts
All issues added to Project with Phase, StepID, PlanHash, ProjectionVersion fields set

process_phases.name: Execution
process_phases.description: Normal development flow. Governs how branches, PRs, and gate checks interact with the plan. Enforces LAW-WORK-001 through LAW-WORK-003.

steps.action: Validate PR linkage on open/edit
checks: Referenced issue number must exist in config/step_issue_linkage.json
steps.failure_action: Post PR comment explaining the requirement; set status check 'pr-linkage-valid' to failure; block merge

steps.action: Set Project item Status to In Progress when PR is opened
steps.notes: Source of Status option IDs: github_field_registry.json

steps.action: Run gate checks as required CI status checks
steps.failure_action: Status check fails; merge blocked by branch protection

steps.action: Seal evidence and update EvidenceState on gate passage
mutations: Post comment on linked issue: 'Evidence sealed: [run_id] — [bundle_path] SHA256: [hash]'

steps.action: On PR merge: close linked issue, set Status to Done
mutations: updateProjectV2ItemFieldValue: Status = 'Done' option ID on the issue project item
GitHub built-in automation closes the issue via 'Closes #N' in PR body

process_phases.name: Phase Advancement
process_phases.description: Evaluates whether the current phase's exit criteria are fully satisfied and, if so, advances the Phase field on all relevant Project items to the next phase. This is the only mechanism that may write the Phase field. Runs on schedule and on key events.

steps.action: Issue GitHub App installation token
Load plan package and derive current phase exit criteria
Query GitHub observed state for current phase
graphql_queries: Fetch all issues in current phase from Project (filter by Phase field)
For each issue: fetch open/closed state, linked PR merge status, EvidenceState field value, GateStatus field value
Fetch milestone completion percentage for current phase milestone(s)

steps.action: Evaluate exit criteria against observed state
evaluation_rules: milestone_complete: milestone percent_complete == 100 (if phase has milestone)

steps.action: Apply phase advancement if decision == ADVANCE
mutations: For each item in next phase's step list: updateProjectV2ItemFieldValue Phase = next_phase option ID
updateProjectV2ItemFieldValue ProjectionVersion++ on all affected items
steps.notes: Phase field on old items is NOT changed; they retain their completed phase. New items get the new phase.

steps.action: Commit decision artifacts

outputs: Phase field updated on Project items (if ADVANCE)

process_phases.name: Reconciliation
process_phases.description: Self-healing loop. Continuously diffs desired state (plan package + step_issue_linkage.json) against observed GitHub state. Classifies drift. Applies convergent corrections for recoverable drift classes. Emits a reconciliation report every run. This process never deletes GitHub Issues; it only flags, corrects field values, and creates missing items.

steps.action: Issue GitHub App installation token
Observe: fetch all Project items and their field values
steps.graphql_query: Paginated query of all projectV2 items with fields: StepID, Phase, Status, PlanHash, ProjectionVersion, EvidenceState, GateStatus, issue open/closed state

steps.action: Diff: compare observed state against desired state

classification_logic.condition: item.StepID in linkage AND item.PlanHash == desired PlanHash AND item.Phase == desired Phase
item.StepID in linkage AND item.PlanHash != desired PlanHash
classification_logic.action: update issue body + PlanHash + ProjectionVersion (re-project this item)

classification_logic.condition: item.StepID in linkage AND automation-only field value does not match desired AND item NOT in override_registry
classification_logic.action: revert field value to desired; post comment on issue noting the revert and who made the change

classification_logic.condition: item.StepID is absent or not in linkage AND item NOT in override_registry
classification_logic.action: open a flag issue titled '[DRIFT-ORPHAN] Issue #{N} has no step_id' referencing the orphan; do NOT close or delete the orphan

classification_logic.condition: step_id in linkage has no corresponding GitHub issue (issue was manually deleted or never created)
classification_logic.action: re-run GH_PROJECTION_APPLY for this step only
skip; log to reconciliation report with override reason

steps.action: Apply corrections and emit report

triggers: push to default branch matching plan_files_glob (OQ-001)

workflows.notes: Replaces TEMP_project_item_sync.yml. Removes PAT dependency. Sources all field IDs from github_field_registry.json.

secrets_required: GITHUB_TOKEN (read-only, for PR validation only; does not touch Projects)

workflows.notes: Updates milestone state. Fires as input event to PROC-3. Upgrade from TEMP: remove PAT, add App auth, source milestone IDs from step_issue_linkage.json rather than env vars.

outputs: token_envelope: {token: string, expires_at: ISO8601, installation_id: string}
program_specs.evidence: token_issuance_log.json (token value redacted, expiry and installation_id retained)
program_specs.implementation_note: Use actions/create-github-app-token@v2 action. Token lifetime <= 1 hour by GitHub policy. Do not cache or reuse across workflow runs.

program_specs.pass_criteria: All required_fields present by name; schema valid; all single-select fields have at least one option
Schema valid per schemas/github_projection_manifest.schema.json; all step_ids unique; all phase references resolve
All steps in manifest have a corresponding entry in step_issue_linkage.json after run
program_specs.idempotency: addProjectV2ItemById returns existing item_id; updateProjectV2ItemFieldValue is a PUT-equivalent; safe to re-run

program_specs.pass_criteria: Report emitted with zero VIOLATION or MISSING items; ORPHAN and STALE items flagged
Decision artifact emitted; if ADVANCE then Phase field update succeeded

artifacts.purpose: Single source of truth for all GitHub Project field IDs and option IDs. Eliminates hardcoded IDs. Must be refreshed whenever fields are added, renamed, or the project is recreated.
artifacts.lifecycle: persisted in repo; committed by bootstrap workflow; never hand-edited

artifacts.purpose: Stable mapping from plan step_id to GitHub Issue number and Project item_id. Resolves Gap 2 (no stable step↔issue linkage). Is the authoritative record of what was projected.
artifacts.lifecycle: persisted in repo; append-only (entries never deleted); committed by projection workflow

artifacts.purpose: The rendered projection manifest. Describes what the GitHub surface should look like based on the current plan: all issues, their fields, milestone assignments, and gate requirements. Regenerated on every plan commit.
artifacts.lifecycle: persisted in repo; overwritten on each projection run; committed

artifacts.purpose: Approved intentional divergences from the plan. Items listed here are skipped by the reconciliation loop. Resolves the escape hatch requirement (ARCH-P-005).
artifacts.writer: human via PR (see OPEN_QUESTIONS OQ-002 for approval process)

overrides.override_reason: Emergency manual phase assignment during incident INCIDENT-2026-04-10

artifacts.lifecycle: persisted in repo; entries expire after expires_at; reconciliation loop should warn on expired entries

artifacts.purpose: Machine-consumable record of the most recent phase advancement evaluation. Required by LAW-GH-003 — no Phase field mutation without this artifact. Stores the decision outcome and all rule evaluations.
artifacts.lifecycle: overwritten on each evaluator run; committed

artifacts.purpose: Human-readable phase advancement report. Summarizes which criteria passed/failed and why. Optional GitHub comment on phase root issue links to this.
artifacts.lifecycle: overwritten on each evaluator run; committed

artifacts.purpose: Output of the most recent reconciliation run. Lists all items by drift class with corrective actions taken.
artifacts.lifecycle: overwritten on each reconciliation run; committed

artifacts.purpose: Append-only log of all reconciliation report summaries. Enables trend analysis and audit trail.
artifacts.lifecycle: append-only; never overwritten; committed after each reconciliation run

artifacts.purpose: Formal definition of field ownership (plan-owned, github-owned, automation-only), drift taxonomy, reconciliation rules, and mutation constraints. The policy document the reconciliation loop enforces.
Defines allowed runner class per program category. Enforces LAW-RUN-001. Declares self-hosted runner labels and fallback strategies.
JSON Schema for ART-001. Validated by GH_FIELDS_VALIDATE before writing the registry.
JSON Schema for ART-003. Validated by GH_PROJECTION_GENERATE.
JSON Schema for ART-005 and ART-006.
JSON Schema for ART-004. Includes required expires_at field to prevent permanent overrides.

gates.description: GitHub App installation token was successfully issued and is not expired.
gates.pass_criteria: token_envelope.json present, token non-empty, expires_at > now + 5 minutes
gates.failure_action: Abort entire workflow. Do not attempt any GitHub API calls.

gates.pass_criteria: jsonschema validation passes with zero errors
gates.failure_action: Abort and require manual bootstrap re-run

gates.description: All required field names are present in the registry.
gates.pass_criteria: All of [Phase, Status, StepID, EvidenceState, DeterminismState, PlanHash, ProjectionVersion, GateStatus] have entries
gates.failure_action: Abort. Required fields are missing from the GitHub Project. Add them before re-running.

gates.description: Composite: GATE_GH_FIELD_REGISTRY_PRESENT AND GATE_GH_FIELD_REGISTRY_SCHEMA_VALID AND GATE_GH_FIELD_REGISTRY_COVERAGE_VALID
config/github_projection_registry.json conforms to schemas/github_projection_manifest.schema.json and all step_ids are unique.
gates.pass_criteria: Schema valid, no duplicate step_ids, all phase references exist in plan

gates.description: Pull request body contains 'Closes #N' or 'Fixes #N' where N is an issue in step_issue_linkage.json.
gates.pass_criteria: Referenced issue number found in step_issue_linkage.json and issue is open
gates.failure_action: Set status check 'pr-linkage-valid' to failure. Post comment on PR.

gates.description: All GitHub Actions required status checks for the active phase have passed.
gates.pass_criteria: Branch protection required checks all show 'success'
gates.failure_action: Merge blocked by GitHub branch protection

gates.description: config/phase_transition_decision.json was written during this evaluator run.
gates.pass_criteria: File present, run_id matches current run
gates.failure_action: Abort PROC-3 without mutating Phase field

gates.description: phase_transition_decision.json outcome field is set to ADVANCE, HOLD, or BLOCKED.
config/reconciliation_report.json was written during this reconciliation run with zero VIOLATION or MISSING items.
gates.pass_criteria: File present, run_id matches current run, violation_count == 0, missing_count == 0
gates.failure_action: Emit report but set workflow exit code to non-zero so CI flags it

authentication_model.token_scope: installation-level, minimum permissions: read/write Projects, read/write Issues, read repository contents

secrets_required.GH_APP_ID: Numeric GitHub App ID. Store as repository or organization secret.
secrets_required.GH_APP_PRIVATE_KEY: PEM-encoded private key for the App. Store as repository or organization secret. Rotate annually.

pat_exception_policy.exception_conditions: Only if GitHub App cannot be installed (e.g., user account without org). Must be declared in policies/github_sync_governance.yaml with named owner and hard expiry date.
pat_exception_policy.saml_sso_note: Organizations with SAML SSO must explicitly authorize PATs. PATs from unrelated users will fail silently on Projects mutations.

authentication_model.github_token_policy: GITHUB_TOKEN may be used only in workflows that do NOT touch Projects v2 fields (e.g., branch-handler.yml PR validation). It cannot access Projects.

graphql_points_budget.github_app_org: 12500 points/hour (scales with org size up to 15000 for GHEC)

backoff_strategy.primary_rate_limit: Read X-RateLimit-Remaining header. If < 10, sleep until X-RateLimit-Reset timestamp.
backoff_strategy.secondary_rate_limit: On HTTP 429: exponential backoff with jitter. Initial delay: 60 seconds. Max delay: 600 seconds. Max retries: 5.
backoff_strategy.concurrency: No more than 1 concurrent workflow writing to the same Project. Use workflow concurrency groups: concurrency: group: github-project-{PROJECT_NUMBER} cancel-in-progress: false

existing_template_disposition.note: TEMP_*.yml files in the workflow inventory are the current baseline. Each entry below describes how it maps to the new design.

changes_required: Remove hardcoded field IDs; source from github_field_registry.json
Replace PAT auth with GitHub App token via GH_AUTH_ISSUE_INSTALLATION_TOKEN
Add step_issue_linkage.json lookup before any Project mutation
Replace PAT auth with GitHub App token
Source milestone IDs from step_issue_linkage.json and field registry, not env vars
Expand from phase sync to full plan projection

templates.notes: Git remote sync is a separate subsystem concern. Not modified by this design. Evaluate independently.
Same as TEMP_remote-sync.yml. Out of scope for this design.

changes_required: Add LAW-WORK-002 enforcement: PR body must reference issue in step_issue_linkage.json
No Projects mutations in this workflow (use GITHUB_TOKEN, not App token)

open_questions.question: What is the canonical path and schema for the plan package that drives projection?
open_questions.detail: PROC-1 (Plan Projection) is triggered when plan files are committed and reads a plan package to generate the projection manifest. The plan package must contain: phase definitions, step contracts (step_id, title, body, gate_requirements), and milestone_group assignments. Currently multiple candidate formats exist: LP_LONG_PLAN phase YAML files, plan_refine_cli output JSON, and hand-authored YAML. Which is authoritative? What is the exact file path glob that triggers plan-projection.yml?
options: plan_refine_cli output at a fixed path (e.g., config/active_plan.json) — deterministic, schema-enforced
LP_LONG_PLAN phase YAML files at a glob pattern — requires a translation step
Hand-authored YAML at a conventional path (e.g., planning/plan.yaml) — simple, manual

open_questions.question: What is the override approval process for github_override_registry.json?
open_questions.detail: Override entries suppress reconciliation corrections for named GitHub items. Without a defined approval process, this becomes an uncontrolled escape hatch. Options: (a) PR required to add an override entry — human review, audit trail, but slow during incidents; (b) workflow_dispatch with required input fields — faster but no peer review; (c) both, with different expiry limits.
options: PR required: any override entry must be merged via PR with at least 1 reviewer approval. Suitable for planned deviations.
workflow_dispatch fast-path: emergency overrides can be issued via workflow_dispatch with a max 24-hour expiry. Must be formalized by PR within 48 hours.
Hybrid: PR for expiry > 24h, workflow_dispatch for expiry <= 24h

open_questions.question: Is a self-hosted Windows runner available and registered, and what is its label?
open_questions.detail: LAW-RUN-001 requires Windows-only operations (PowerShell workflows, local filesystem sync, Windows toolchain) to run on a self-hosted Windows runner. If no such runner is registered, workflows that declare windows runner class will queue for up to 24 hours before timing out. If unavailable, those workflows must either be restructured to run on ubuntu-latest or deferred.
options: Self-hosted runner available: provide label name, OS, and availability guarantee
Not available: audit which workflows actually require Windows; restructure or defer those workflows
Not available now, planned: document which workflows are blocked pending runner setup
open_questions.decision_needed_by: Before implementing any workflow with self_hosted_windows runner class

open_questions.question: What is the sub-issue policy for step contracts with nested sub-steps?
open_questions.detail: The mapping defines 'sub-steps may become sub-issues up to 2 levels'. GitHub supports 8 levels, but deep nesting complicates Phase field propagation and reconciliation. The design states one step = one issue but does not define when sub-issues are created vs when additional top-level issues are created for sub-steps.
options: Flat: all steps are top-level issues regardless of nesting in the plan. Simplest for reconciliation.
One level of sub-issues: step contract = parent issue; sub-steps = sub-issues. Phase field on parent only.
Full nesting up to 2 levels: plan hierarchy maps directly. Phase field set on all levels.

open_questions.question: What happens to a GitHub Issue when its corresponding step is removed from the plan?
open_questions.detail: When a step_id is removed from the plan, its entry remains in step_issue_linkage.json. The reconciliation loop will not flag it as MISSING (it still has a step_id). But the plan no longer references it. Should the issue be: (a) archived in the Project; (b) closed with an automation comment; (c) left open and flagged as STALE-REMOVED; (d) left untouched (manual decision)?
options: Close and comment: automation closes issue with comment 'Step removed from plan at [commit]'
Archive in Project: remove from active board view but do not close
Flag only: reconciliation classifies as STALE-REMOVED and flags it; human decides
No action: step removal is a human-managed operation

open_questions.question: Where is the GitHub App installed — organization-level or repository-level?
open_questions.detail: Organization projects require org-level App installation with read/write organization projects permission. User projects require user-level installation. The installation scope determines the GraphQL query shape (organization vs user) and the minimum App permissions.
options: Organization project: install App at org level, use organization(login: $org) GraphQL root
User project: install App at user level, use user(login: $user) GraphQL root

open_questions.question: What is the reconciliation interval, and is it configurable per environment?
open_questions.detail: The current design specifies cron '*/15 * * * *' (every 15 minutes). This means up to 96 runs per day. At 1 GraphQL query per item in the project plus 1 per field read, with 50+ items and 8 fields each, this is significant API consumption. For large projects this may approach rate limits. The interval should be configurable, with a recommendation per project size.
options: Fixed 15 minutes: simple, matches the design intent
Configurable via repository variable RECONCILIATION_INTERVAL_MINUTES: default 15, allows tuning
Adaptive: start at 15 min, back off to 60 min when zero drift is observed for 3 consecutive runs

open_questions.question: How is the initial phase determined when an issue is first created by projection?
open_questions.detail: When GH_PROJECTION_APPLY creates a new issue, it sets the Phase field to the step's assigned phase from the plan. However, if the plan's current active phase is PH-02 and a new step is added to PH-01 (backfill), should the Phase field be set to PH-01 (plan value) or the current active phase? The answer determines whether projection and advancement are fully independent operations.
options: Always use plan value: Phase = the phase the step belongs to per plan. Advancement evaluator reads this correctly.
Use min(plan value, current active phase): prevents projecting items into phases already completed.
Flag as ANOMALY and require manual resolution when projected phase < current active phase

implementation_order.note: Derived from dependency graph. No item should be implemented before all its blockers are resolved.

deliverables: Resolve OQ-001, OQ-003, OQ-006 (all BLOCKING open questions)
critical_path.reason: These three decisions unblock the entire implementation
Schemas must exist before any artifact can be validated. Policy files define what everything else enforces.
PROC-0 is the prerequisite for all other process phases. Nothing else runs until the field registry is valid.
PROC-1 creates the issue corpus and linkage table that all downstream processes depend on.
PROC-2 execution workflows. Depend on step_issue_linkage.json being populated.
PROC-4 self-healing loop. Requires resolved OQ-002, OQ-005, OQ-007 before finalizing.
PROC-3 phase advancement. Depends on PROC-2 producing EvidenceState values correctly.
